architecture.summary: GenPRM is a generative process reward model that reads intermediate reasoning steps from a base LLM and outputs explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoning with code verification before scoring each step. The model receives step trajectories as text input, generates verification reasoning and code outputs as intermediate tokens, and produces a final judgment score. A trained bridge component (rationale synthesis with code verification) transforms external verification signals into generative targets that the model learns to produce.
trained_components: generative process reward model (text generation head for reasoning and code)
rationale synthesis framework with code verification

biblio.title: GenPRM: Scaling Test-Time Compute of Process Reward Models via Generative Reasoning

claims.statement: GenPRM significantly outperforms prior PRMs with only 23K training data from MATH dataset
A 1.5B GenPRM outperforms GPT-4o through test-time scaling
A 7B GenPRM surpasses Qwen2.5-Math-PRM-72B on ProcessBench
GenPRM performs explicit Chain-of-Thought reasoning with code verification before providing judgment for each reasoning step
GenPRM demonstrates strong abilities to serve as a critic model for policy model refinement
Relative Progress Estimation (RPE) and a rationale synthesis framework that incorporates code verification are used to obtain high-quality process supervision labels and rationale data
GenPRM leverages the generative abilities of LLMs rather than depending on scalar value prediction
GenPRM addresses the inability to scale test-time compute of prior PRMs
